item.title: Renowned U.K. artist David Hockney dies at 88
item.description: Hockney was a globally celebrated painter who helped lead the Pop art movement in the 1960s, spent time in California, and defiantly refused to give up smoking.

item.title: Iran cautions no "final conclusion" yet as Trump touts deal to end war
item.description: Iran said Friday it had not yet reached a final conclusion on a deal to end the war with the U.S. despite President Trump announcing an agreement would be signed soon.

item.title: Mexican reporter killed, another journalist kidnapped from her home
item.description: Luis Angel Lopez Valdez was killed in Veracruz just days after armed assailants abducted journalist Roxana Guzman from her home.

item.title: Taylor Swift is youngest woman inducted into Songwriters Hall of Fame, at 36
item.description: Taylor Swift became the youngest woman ever inducted into the Songwriters Hall of Fame Thursday night, at the age of 36.

item.title: 1 killed in Iowa as severe storms, suspected tornadoes slam Midwest
item.description: Severe storms that swept through the Midwest late Thursday knocked out power to hundreds of thousands of customers, damaged buildings and canceled flights.

item.title: China arrests U.S. researcher on suspicion of spying
item.description: China says U.S. national U Min Zin, founder of a think tank focused on Myanmar, was detained on suspicion of "espionage and endangering China's national security."

item.title: Thai princess dies at age 47 after 3 years in hospital
item.description: Thailand's Princess Bajrakitiyabha Mahidol, a lawyer and the eldest of the king's seven children, has died at 47 after three years in a hospital, royal officials said. She was an advocate for women's rights.

item.title: Trump says "settlement" reached on Iran, signing could happen this weekend
item.description: President Trump said earlier Thursday he called off new military strikes on Iran, hours after threatening to escalate the war.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Supreme Court declines to let Alabama move forward with nitrogen gas execution
item.description: The Supreme Court declined a request from Alabama to move forward with a scheduled execution using nitrogen hypoxia, with Justices Clarence Thomas, Samuel Alito and Neil Gorsuch dissenting.

item.title: White House to appeal judge's order to remove Trump's name from Kennedy Center
item.description: The board move marks a shift from a June 4 memo to staff saying email signatures, letterhead and other documents must reflect the name as "The John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts" or "Kennedy Center."

item.title: How Americans are coping with rising costs
item.description: As the war in Iran continues and prices keep rising, CBS News is following stories about everyday Americans finding new ways to cope with the increasing costs.

item.title: More employers seeking critical thinking skills amid AI rise
item.description: As artificial intelligence cements its role across more U.S. job sectors, career training experts say the technology is actually reviving the need for liberal arts skills and diminishing the need for more traditional technical skills. CBS News MoneyWatch reporter Megan Cerullo has more.

item.title: Midwest battered by dangerous storms, 1 killed in Des Moines
item.description: Severe weather broke out in the Midwest on Thursday, day three of a multi-day threat. More than 120 million people are in the path of potentially dangerous storms. In Des Moines, a man was killed after a tree broke apart and fell on him as storms passed through. Rob Marciano reports and has the forecast.

item.title: SpaceX's first employee remains on mission: "Space is super important"
item.description: Tom Mueller, Elon Musk's first hire at SpaceX, expects the company's IPO to help power a new era in space exploration.

item.title: Proposed data center near Nashville Zoo sparks heavy pushback
item.description: Residents packed a public hearing in Nashville, Tennessee, on Thursday, looking to stop a nearly 70,000-square-foot data center from being built near the Nashville Zoo.

item.title: What comes next if an Iran agreement is finalized?
item.description: President Trump announced Thursday that the U.S.had reached a "settlement" with Iran that would begin talks to possibly end the war. Andrew Borene, a senior fellow at the National Security Institute and a former senior intelligence official, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: How Burt Jones, Rick Jackson are differentiating themselves to voters in Georgia
item.description: President Trump is joining Republican candidate for Georgia governor Burt Jones on Tuesday night for a tele-rally ahead of the state's runoff election. Jones, Georgia's lieutenant governor, is facing billionaire Rick Jackson as the two look to succeed term-limited Republican Gov. Brian Kemp. CBS News' Fin Gómez has more.

item.title: What we know about a possible agreement with Iran
item.description: President Trump said the U.S. is closer than ever to an agreement with Iran. The agreement is not final, but President Trump said in the Oval Office that there could be a signing ceremony as early as this weekend. CBS News' Natalie Brand and Eleanor Watson have more.

item.title: Details on Trump's potential deal with Iran
item.description: President Trump has promised a deal with Iran several times over the past two months. Margaret Brennan reports on what's included this time.

item.title: She confided in ChatGPT the night of her suicide. Now, her mother is suing OpenAI.
item.description: A mother has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging the chatbot's design led to her daughter's suicide.

item.title: U.S.-Iran memo of understanding likely to be signed next week, sources say
item.description: The signing of the memorandum or letter of intent would kick off 60 days of talks to negotiate details of an enduring U.S.-Iran agreement.

item.title: 6/11: CBS Evening News
item.description: President Trump cancels the third day of strikes against Iran; Trump says a peace deal with Iran will be "finalized" soon.

item.title: Human remains found in 2 Twin Cities lakes 33 years ago identified
item.description: Investigators in Washington County, Minnesota, say they've identified the human remains found in two Twin Cities lakes more than three decades ago.

item.title: Markings that appear to show "86 47" seen on National Mall
item.description: A photographer from the Reuters news outlet saw the apparent "86 47" markings from atop the Washington Monument.

item.title: The World Cup will draw millions of fans, but economic payoff is modest
item.description: Although the five-week soccer tournament starting on Thursday is the largest sporting event ever, the U.S. economic gains are likely to be muted.

item.title: Karmelo Anthony's family says "nobody wins" after murder verdict
item.description: Karmelo Anthony's mother Kala Hayes told CBS News that her son "didn't mean to hurt anyone" and "was defending himself" when he stabbed another student, Austin Metcalf.

item.title: See when the U.S. men's soccer team is playing in 2026 World Cup
item.description: The U.S. men's national soccer team, which last appeared at the 2022 World Cup, will face Paraguay to kick off its 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Poll: Most believe extraterrestrial life exists – and some think it's already here
item.description: Many have watched recently released UFO videos, but most still think the government knows more than it is saying.

item.title: Knicks rally from 29 points down to beat Spurs 107-106
item.description: The Knicks set the record for the biggest NBA Finals comeback Wednesday night as they rallied from 29 points down to beat the Spurs 107-106.
